steps.expected: hashToken() called before storing refresh token
steps.observed: All three files call hashToken() on the refresh token before prisma.user.update

steps.action: Register user via curl and query DB
steps.expected: DB stores hex hash, not raw JWT
steps.observed: DB refreshToken column contains 64-char hex string, not the raw JWT returned in response

evidence.codeInspection: token-hash.ts: createHash('sha256').update(token).digest('hex'); register.ts: hashToken(tokens.refreshToken); login.ts: hashToken(tokens.refreshToken); refresh.ts: hashToken(refreshToken) for comparison and hashToken(tokens.refreshToken) for new storage

steps.observed: catch block returns reply.status(401).send(...), preventing route handler from continuing

evidence.codeInspection: plugins/auth.ts: catch { return reply.status(401).send({...}); } — the return statement prevents further execution

steps.expected: Dummy bcrypt compare when user not found
steps.observed: DUMMY_HASH constant defined; when !user, bcrypt.compare(password, DUMMY_HASH) is called before returning 401

evidence.codeInspection: login.ts: const DUMMY_HASH = '$2b$12$LJ3m4ys3Lg2VBe0A8MrXxu7N6OVxB5VH6kFGz3K8HnGFbkOKMgK.i'; if (!user) { await bcrypt.compare(password, DUMMY_HASH); return reply.status(401).send(INVALID_CREDENTIALS_ERROR); }

steps.action: Register first user with brandName 'SecFlow1 Brand'
Register second user with same brandName 'SecFlow1 Brand'
steps.observed: 409 with message 'A brand with this name already exists'

evidence.codeInspection: register.ts: catch block checks error.code === 'P2002' and returns 409; curl test confirmed 409 response on duplicate brand name
server.ts: conditional guard `if (config.NODE_ENV !== 'production')` wraps both fastifySwagger and fastifySwaggerUi registration

steps.observed: fastify.prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => { ... }) wraps findUnique, comparison, generateTokenPair, and update

evidence.codeInspection: refresh.ts: const result = await fastify.prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => { const user = await tx.user.findUnique(...); if (!user || user.refreshToken !== hashedIncoming) return null; const tokens = generateTokenPair(...); await tx.user.update(...); return { user, tokens }; });
auth.ts: export const passwordSchema = z.string().min(8, '...').max(128, 'Password must be at most 128 characters'); curl test with 200-char password returned 400

summary: Tested 7 API security assertions via code inspection and curl tests. All 7 passed.
